Transfer of FunctionsUnited States Information Agency (other than Broadcasting Board of Governors and International Broadcasting Bureau) abolished and functions transferred to Secretary of State, see sections 6531 and 6532 of this title.

Ex. Ord. No. 12703. Support for East European Democracy (SEED) ProgramEx. Ord. No. 12703, , 55 F.R. 6351, as amended by Ex. Ord. No. 13118, § 10(2), , 64 F.R. 16598, provided:Feb. 20, 1990Mar. 31, 1999By the authority vested in me as President by the Constitution and laws of the United States of America, including the Support for East European Democracy (SEED) Act (, hereinafter referred to as the “Act”) [ et seq.] and of the United States Code, it is hereby ordered as follows:P.L. 101–17922 U.S.C. 5401section 301 of Title 31. . The functions conferred by Title VII of the Act [ et seq.] relating to reports to the Congress are hereby delegated to the Coordinator of the SEED Program. The Coordinator is authorized to assign responsibility for particular aspects of the reports to appropriate agencies.SectionSEED Program Coordinator22 U.S.C. 5471Sec. 2. . The functions conferred upon the President by section 201 of the Act [] relating to Enterprise Funds for Poland and Hungary are hereby delegated to the Secretary of State.Department of State22 U.S.C. 5421. 3. . The functions conferred upon the President by section 602 of the Act [] regarding the establishment of a SEED Information Center System in cooperation with the Governments of Poland and Hungary are hereby delegated to the Secretary of Commerce. This authority is to be exercised in consultation with the SEED Program Coordinator and in consultation with other agencies as appropriate.SecDepartment of Commerce22 U.S.C. 5462. 4. . The functions conferred upon the President by section 104 of the Act [] regarding debt reduction of certain East European countries are hereby delegated to the Secretary of the Treasury. The Secretary shall consult, as appropriate, with other relevant agencies in exercising the functions herein delegated.SecDepartment of the Treasury22 U.S.C. 5414George Bush.

children.heading: SEED Program
children.label: a SEED Program
children.text: The United States shall implement, beginning in fiscal year 1990, a concerted Program of Support for East European Democracy (which may also be referred to as the “SEED Program”). The SEED Program shall be comprised of diverse undertakings designed to provide cost-effective assistance to those countries of Eastern Europe that have taken substantive steps toward institutionalizing political democracy and economic pluralism.

children.label: A
children.text: the establishment of fully democratic and representative political systems based on free and fair elections,

children.label: B
children.text: effective recognition of fundamental liberties and individual freedoms, including freedom of speech, religion, and association,

children.label: C
children.text: termination of all laws and regulations which impede the operation of a free press and the formation of political parties,

children.label: D
children.text: creation of an independent judiciary, and

children.label: E
children.text: establishment of non-partisan military, security, and police forces;

children.label: A
children.text: privatization of economic entities,

children.label: B
children.text: establishment of full rights to acquire and hold private property, including land and the benefits of contractual relations,

children.label: C
children.text: simplification of regulatory controls regarding the establishment and operation of businesses,

children.label: D
children.text: dismantlement of all wage and price controls,

children.label: E
children.text: removal of trade restrictions, including on both imports and exports,

children.label: F
children.text: liberalization of investment and capital, including the repatriation of profits by foreign investors;

children.label: G
children.text: tax policies which provide incentives for economic activity and investment,

children.label: H
children.text: establishment of rights to own and operate private banks and other financial service firms, as well as unrestricted access to private sources of credit, and

children.label: I
children.text: access to a market for stocks, bonds, and other instruments through which individuals may invest in the private sector; and

children.label: A
children.text: to Communist or other political parties or organizations which are not committed to respect for the democratic process, or

children.label: B
children.text: to the defense or security forces of any member country of the Warsaw Pact.

children.heading: Leadership in the World Bank and International Monetary Fund
children.label: 1 Leadership in the World Bank and International Monetary Fund
A
children.text: loans by the International Bank for Reconstruction and Development and its affiliated institutions in the World Bank group that are designed to modernize industry, agriculture, and infrastructure, and

children.label: B
children.text: International Monetary Fund programs designed to stimulate sound economic growth.

children.heading: Currency stabilization loans
children.label: 2 Currency stabilization loans
children.text: United States leadership in supporting multilateral agreement to provide government-to-government loans for currency stabilization where such loans can reduce inflation and thereby foster conditions necessary for the effective implementation of economic reforms.

children.heading: Debt reduction and rescheduling
children.label: 3 Debt reduction and rescheduling
children.text: Participation in multilateral activities aimed at reducing and rescheduling a country’s international debt, when reduction and deferral of debt payments can assist the process of political and economic transition.

children.heading: Agricultural assistance
children.label: 4 Agricultural assistance
children.text: Assistance through the grant and concessional sale of food and other agricultural commodities and products when such assistance can ease critical shortages but not inhibit agricultural production and marketing in the recipient country.

children.heading: Enterprise Funds
children.label: 5 Enterprise Funds
children.text: Grants to support private, nonprofit “Enterprise Funds”, designated by the President pursuant to law and governed by a Board of Directors, which undertake loans, grants, equity investments, feasibility studies, technical assistance, training, and other forms of assistance to private enterprise activities in the Eastern European country for which the Enterprise Fund so is designated.

children.heading: Labor market-oriented technical assistance
children.label: 6 Labor market-oriented technical assistance
children.text: Technical assistance programs directed at promoting labor market reforms and facilitating economic adjustment.

children.heading: Technical training
children.label: 7 Technical training
children.text: Programs to provide technical skills to assist in the development of a market economy.

children.heading: Peace Corps
children.label: 8 Peace Corps
children.text: Establishment of Peace Corps programs.

children.heading: Support for indigenous credit unions
children.label: 9 Support for indigenous credit unions
children.text: Support for the establishment of indigenous credit unions.

children.heading: Generalized System of Preferences
children.label: 10 Generalized System of Preferences
children.text: Eligibility for trade benefits under the Generalized System of Preferences.

children.heading: Normal trade relations
children.label: 11 Normal trade relations
children.text: The granting of temporary or permanent nondiscriminatory treatment to the products of an East European country through the application of the criteria and procedures established by (commonly referred to as the “Jackson-Vanik amendment”).section 2432 of title 19

children.heading: United States International Development Finance Corporation
children.label: 12 United States International Development Finance Corporation
children.text: Programs of the United States International Development Finance Corporation.

children.heading: Export-Import Bank programs
children.label: 13 Export-Import Bank programs
children.text: Programs of the Export-Import Bank of the United States.

children.heading: Trade and Development Program activities
children.label: 14 Trade and Development Program activities
children.text: Trade and Development Agency activities under the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 [ et seq.].22 U.S.C. 2151

children.heading: Investment treaties
children.label: 15 Investment treaties
children.text: Negotiation of bilateral investment treaties.

children.heading: Special tax treatment of below-market loans
children.label: 16 Special tax treatment of below-market loans
children.text: Exempting bonds from Internal Revenue Code [title 26] rules relating to below-market loans.

children.heading: Exchange activities
children.label: 17 Exchange activities
children.text: Expanded exchange activities under the Fulbright, International Visitors, and other programs conducted by the United States Information Agency.

children.heading: Cultural centers
children.label: 18 Cultural centers
children.text: Contributions toward the establishment of reciprocal cultural centers that can facilitate educational and cultural exchange and expanded understanding of Western social democracy.

children.heading: Sister institutions
children.label: 19 Sister institutions
children.text: Establishment of sister institution programs between American and East European schools and universities, towns and cities, and other organizations in such fields as medicine and health care, business management, environmental protection, and agriculture.

children.heading: Scholarships
children.label: 20 Scholarships
children.text: Scholarships to enable students to study in the United States.

children.heading: Science and technology exchanges
children.label: 21 Science and technology exchanges
children.text: Grants for the implementation of bilateral agreements providing for cooperation in science and technology exchange.

children.heading: Assistance for democratic institutions
children.label: 22 Assistance for democratic institutions
children.text: Assistance designed to support the development of legal, legislative, electoral, journalistic, and other institutions of free, pluralist societies.

children.heading: Environmental assistance
children.label: 23 Environmental assistance
children.text: Environmental assistance directed at overcoming crucial deficiencies in air and water quality and other determinants of a healthful society.

children.heading: Medical assistance
children.label: 24 Medical assistance
children.text: Medical assistance specifically targeted to overcome severe deficiencies in pharmaceuticals and other basic health supplies.

children.heading: Encouragement for private investment and voluntary assistance
children.label: 25 Encouragement for private investment and voluntary assistance
children.text: Encouraging private investment and voluntary private assistance, using a variety of means including a SEED Information Center System and the provision by the Department of Defense of transportation for private nonfinancial contributions.
